must_detect.matched_text:
Inverter plant capex of INR 68 crore with vendor Kairox Fabrication LLP (GSTIN: 27‑Z X Q P K‑1 2 3 4‑Z‑9) is issuer‑material (>10% of gross block) and presently unpublished; classify as U P S I, circulate strictly on need‑to‑know and log in SDD (Entry ID: AUR‑SDD‑20 26/05/29‑C2).
Tentative Q2 FY27 price list revision (net +3.5% on Series ‘SolLift’ in‑verter line) has an expected revenue impact >5% based on internal projections; treat as UPSI until approved and disclosed.
Intimations under Reg 29 and the outcome under Reg 30/33 will be filed on NSE NEAPS and BSE Listing Centre within 30 minutes of Board conclusion; no selective disclosure to analysts or vendors is permitted.
Public-status check: MoU with Nayanta Renewables Pvt. Ltd. was disclosed via press release dated 15 Apr 2026 and exchange filing Ref ATL/LODR/2026‑04‑15 (see http://investors.aurora-technologics.example.in/filings?ref=ATL‑20 26‑PR‑0415), hence it is not UPSI.
For the avoidance of doubt, this note does not announce any MAC and does not alter the already public information that the NSE holiday calendar lists 17 June 2026 as a trading holiday (public domain, not issuer‑specific).
DPDPA compliance: the ‘Project Prāṇa’ pilot captured names, emails, and districts; any Aadhaar, PAN, or GSTIN inadvertently entered in free‑text (e.g., P A N: Z X Q P K 1 2 3 4 J) must be purged; implement consent logs and data minimisation.
Children’s data: the factory open‑day RSVP includes minors (e.g., Master Neel Rathod, 13 years); obtain verifiable parental/guardian consent before processing and do not include child details in marketing lists.
Employee onboarding forms must not request Aadhaar photocopies; mask identifiers in payroll and restrict access to HR only; privacy queries: privacy@aurora-technologics.example.in.
Analyst interaction on 14 June 2026 will confine to what is already in the public domain; if any UPSI is to be discussed, ensure simultaneous disclosure to NSE/BSE and the Company website as per SEBI PIT practice note.
Designated Persons are reminded not to trade in Company securities during the blackout window and to pre‑clear post‑window trades; maintain the Structured Digital Database with date‑time stamps and recipients.
Contact: Company Secretary Ms. Rhea Kulkarni (secy@aurora-technologics.example.in) or Shri Devansh Nair, Chair–Audit Committee; for board hard‑copies see h t t p : / / intranet.aurora‑

uncertain.reason: Consent logging is a DPDPA compliance control but there is no explicit allowed rule for generic consent markers beyond withdrawal.
Legally relevant for children’s data under DPDPA, but no specific allowed rule for general consent (only withdrawal).
Minimisation/redaction control is relevant but not anchored to the explicit 'data minimisation' phrase for the allowed rule.
Access restriction is a privacy/security control but not covered by the allowed rules set.
